result.summary: Box exposes a REST HTTP API at https://api.box.com/2.0, a public hosted MCP endpoint, and an npm-delivered CLI (`box` / `npx box`).
result.description: Box is a cloud content management and collaboration platform for files, folders, search, workflows, signatures, and AI features. Its developer platform lets applications and agents work with Box content and enterprise features programmatically.

box_access_token.label: Box access token
box_access_token.setup: Create a Box app in the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console) or generate a short-lived developer token there. Box docs state every API endpoint requires a valid access token, sent as `Authorization: Bearer ...`. Easiest path for testing: open your app’s **Configuration** tab and click **Generate Developer Token** as shown in the [Developer Tokens guide](https://box.dev/guides/authentication/tokens/developer-tokens). For production, configure OAuth 2.0, JWT, or Client Credentials on your app from the same console per the [authentication overview](https://box.dev/guides/authentication).

box_oauth_app.label: Box OAuth 2.0 app credentials
box_oauth_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a **Platform App** and choose **OAuth 2.0** as the app type per [Setup with OAuth 2.0](https://box.dev/guides/authentication/oauth2/oauth2-setup). Configure your `redirect_uri`, select scopes, save, and copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app configuration. Use those credentials in the standard Box OAuth flow to obtain user access tokens.

box_ccg_app.label: Box Client Credentials Grant app credentials
box_ccg_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a **Platform App** and choose **Client Credentials Grant** as the app type per [Setup with Client Credentials Grant](https://box.dev/guides/authentication/client-credentials/client-credentials-setup). Authorize the app if your enterprise requires admin approval, then copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app configuration. Use them to exchange for Box access tokens server-side.

box_jwt_app.label: Box JWT app configuration
box_jwt_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a **Platform App** and choose **Server Auth - JWT** as the app type per [Setup with JWT](https://box.dev/guides/authentication/jwt/jwt-setup). Generate or upload the app keypair, authorize the app if required, then download the app’s JSON configuration and keep the private key available. This app configuration is used to mint Box access tokens server-side.

box_mcp_integration.label: Box MCP Integration Credentials
box_mcp_integration.setup: A Box admin enables the hosted MCP server in the Box Admin Console, then creates **Integration Credentials** for **Box MCP server** as described in [Set up the MCP server](https://box.dev/guides/box-mcp/setup). In **Integrations**, configure **Box MCP server**, click **+ Add Integration Credentials**, then copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret**, set the external client’s `redirect_uri`, and choose scopes. These credentials are used by MCP clients with Box’s OAuth endpoints.

evidence: Seed fact: MCP server endpoint detected with auth none
